deck.title: IT/Tech Essentials
deck.description: 30 hand-picked IT and software engineering vocabulary words covering CEFR A2-B2.

cards.definition: To put a plan, decision, or agreement into effect; in software, to write the actual code for a feature or function.
cards.exampleSentence: We need to implement the authentication module before the sprint ends.
cards.exampleTranslation: Chúng ta cần phải triển khai module xác thực trước khi sprint kết thúc.
cards.etymology: From Latin implementum 'filling up', from implere 'to fill'.

cards.definition: To release and make software available in a production or staging environment.
cards.exampleSentence: We deploy the latest build to the server every Friday afternoon.
cards.exampleTranslation: Chúng tôi triển khai bản build mới nhất lên máy chủ vào chiều thứ Sáu hàng tuần.
cards.etymology: From French déployer 'to unfold, spread out', from Latin displicare.

cards.definition: To restructure existing code without changing its external behavior, improving readability and maintainability.
cards.exampleSentence: The team decided to refactor the legacy payment service to reduce technical debt.
cards.exampleTranslation: Nhóm quyết định tái cấu trúc dịch vụ thanh toán cũ để giảm nợ kỹ thuật.
cards.etymology: From re- + factor, coined by Martin Fowler in software engineering context (1990s).

cards.definition: To identify, locate, and fix errors or bugs in software code.
cards.exampleSentence: She spent three hours trying to debug the null pointer exception in production.
cards.exampleTranslation: Cô ấy đã mất ba giờ cố gắng debug lỗi null pointer exception trên môi trường sản xuất.
cards.etymology: From de- 'remove' + bug (defect). Grace Hopper famously taped a real moth into a logbook in 1947.

cards.definition: To mark a software feature or API as outdated and discouraged, signaling it will be removed in a future version.
cards.exampleSentence: The old login endpoint has been deprecated; please migrate to the new OAuth flow.
cards.exampleTranslation: Endpoint đăng nhập cũ đã bị khai tử; vui lòng chuyển sang luồng OAuth mới.
cards.etymology: From Latin deprecari 'to pray against, avert by prayer'; in computing sense since 1980s.

cards.definition: Capable of handling increased workload or user demand without performance degradation.
cards.exampleSentence: We chose a microservices architecture because it is more scalable than a monolith.
cards.exampleTranslation: Chúng tôi chọn kiến trúc microservices vì nó có khả năng mở rộng tốt hơn monolith.
cards.etymology: From scale + -able; scale from Latin scala 'ladder, steps'.

cards.definition: The delay between a request being sent and the response being received; a measure of network or system responsiveness.
cards.exampleSentence: High latency in the API was causing the user interface to feel sluggish.
cards.exampleTranslation: Độ trễ cao trong API khiến giao diện người dùng cảm thấy chậm chạp.
cards.etymology: From Latin latentia 'concealment', from latere 'to lurk, lie hidden'.

cards.definition: The amount of data processed or transactions completed by a system in a given period of time.
cards.exampleSentence: After optimizing the database queries, throughput increased by 40 percent.
cards.exampleTranslation: Sau khi tối ưu các truy vấn cơ sở dữ liệu, thông lượng tăng 40 phần trăm.
cards.etymology: Compound of through + put; emerged in computing contexts in the 1950s.

cards.definition: Describing an operation that produces the same result no matter how many times it is applied.
cards.exampleSentence: PUT requests should be idempotent; sending the same request twice must not create duplicates.
cards.exampleTranslation: Các yêu cầu PUT phải là idempotent; gửi cùng một yêu cầu hai lần không được tạo ra bản sao.
cards.etymology: From Latin idem 'same' + potent 'powerful'; adopted in mathematics and computing.

cards.definition: Occurring independently of the main program flow, allowing other operations to continue while waiting for a task to complete.
cards.exampleSentence: JavaScript uses asynchronous callbacks to handle network requests without blocking the main thread.
cards.exampleTranslation: JavaScript sử dụng các hàm gọi lại bất đồng bộ để xử lý các yêu cầu mạng mà không cần khóa luồng chính.
cards.etymology: From Greek a- 'not' + synchronos 'happening at the same time'.

cards.definition: A storage location for software packages, code, or data; also a design pattern that abstracts data access.
cards.exampleSentence: All source code changes must be committed to the Git repository before review.
cards.exampleTranslation: Tất cả các thay đổi mã nguồn phải được commit vào kho Git trước khi review.
cards.etymology: From Latin repositorium 'a storehouse', from reponere 'to store'.

cards.definition: An external library, module, or service that a software project relies on to function correctly.
cards.exampleSentence: Before starting the project, install all dependencies listed in the package.json file.
cards.exampleTranslation: Trước khi bắt đầu dự án, hãy cài đặt tất cả các phụ thuộc được liệt kê trong file package.json.
cards.etymology: From Latin dependentia, from dependere 'to hang from'.

cards.definition: A pre-built structure of code that provides a foundation for building software applications.
cards.exampleSentence: The team chose Next.js as the frontend framework for the new product.
cards.exampleTranslation: Nhóm đã chọn Next.js làm framework frontend cho sản phẩm mới.
cards.etymology: Compound of frame + work; in software sense from 1980s.

cards.definition: Software that acts as a bridge between an operating system or database and applications, often handling authentication, logging, or request transformation.
cards.exampleSentence: The authentication middleware checks the JWT token on every protected API route.
cards.exampleTranslation: Middleware xác thực kiểm tra token JWT trên mọi tuyến API được bảo vệ.
cards.etymology: Compound of middle + ware; coined in the 1960s for software between OS and applications.

cards.definition: The data carried by a network request or message, excluding headers and metadata.
cards.exampleSentence: The API returns a JSON payload containing the user profile and access token.
cards.exampleTranslation: API trả về một payload JSON chứa thông tin hồ sơ người dùng và token truy cập.
cards.etymology: From pay + load; originally the profitable part of a cargo load (1920s); adopted in networking.

cards.definition: The ability of different objects to be treated as instances of the same type through a shared interface, allowing the same operation to behave differently.
cards.exampleSentence: Polymorphism lets us call the render method on any shape without knowing its concrete type.
cards.exampleTranslation: Tính đa hình cho phép chúng ta gọi phương thức render trên bất kỳ hình dạng nào mà không cần biết kiểu cụ thể của nó.
cards.etymology: From Greek polys 'many' + morphe 'form'; used in biology before computing.

cards.definition: An OOP principle of bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, restricting direct access from outside.
cards.exampleSentence: Good encapsulation hides internal state and exposes only what is necessary through a public interface.
cards.exampleTranslation: Tính đóng gói tốt che giấu trạng thái nội bộ và chỉ lộ ra những gì cần thiết qua giao diện công khai.
cards.etymology: From Latin capsula 'small box'; en- prefix means 'to put into'.

cards.definition: A defined limit or boundary value that triggers a specific action when crossed.
cards.exampleSentence: The alert fires when CPU usage exceeds the threshold of 90 percent.
cards.exampleTranslation: Cảnh báo được kích hoạt khi mức sử dụng CPU vượt qua ngưỡng 90 phần trăm.
cards.etymology: Old English therscold 'the sill of a door'; origin uncertain, possibly related to threshing grain.

cards.definition: Describing components or data that are duplicated to provide backup or improve reliability; also code that is unnecessary.
cards.exampleSentence: We set up redundant servers in two data centers to ensure high availability.
cards.exampleTranslation: Chúng tôi thiết lập các máy chủ dự phòng tại hai trung tâm dữ liệu để đảm bảo tính sẵn sàng cao.
cards.etymology: From Latin redundare 'to overflow'; re- + undare 'to surge (as waves)'.

cards.definition: Broken down into small, detailed, and precise components or levels of control.
cards.exampleSentence: The new permission system provides granular control over which users can access each resource.
cards.exampleTranslation: Hệ thống phân quyền mới cung cấp khả năng kiểm soát chi tiết về việc người dùng nào có thể truy cập từng tài nguyên.
cards.etymology: From Latin granulum 'small grain', diminutive of granum 'grain'.

cards.definition: Describing data or state that is saved across sessions and survives system restarts.
cards.exampleSentence: User preferences are stored in a persistent database rather than session memory.
cards.exampleTranslation: Tùy chọn người dùng được lưu trữ trong cơ sở dữ liệu bền vững thay vì bộ nhớ phiên làm việc.
cards.etymology: From Latin persistere 'to stand firm'; per- 'through' + sistere 'to cause to stand'.

cards.definition: Short-lived or temporary; in computing, describes resources such as containers or tokens that are destroyed after use.
cards.exampleSentence: Ephemeral containers are spun up for each test run and discarded immediately afterward.
cards.exampleTranslation: Các container tạm thời được khởi tạo cho mỗi lần chạy thử nghiệm và bị loại bỏ ngay sau đó.
cards.etymology: From Greek ephemeros 'lasting only a day'; epi- 'on' + hemera 'day'.

cards.definition: Capable of being changed or modified after creation.
cards.exampleSentence: Avoid mutable global state because it makes the program harder to reason about.
cards.exampleTranslation: Tránh trạng thái toàn cục có thể thay đổi vì nó khiến chương trình khó suy luận hơn.
cards.etymology: From Latin mutabilis 'changeable', from mutare 'to change'.

cards.definition: Unable to be changed after creation; immutable data structures improve predictability and thread safety.
cards.exampleSentence: In React, treating state as immutable prevents subtle bugs caused by direct mutation.
cards.exampleTranslation: Trong React, việc coi state là bất biến ngăn ngừa các lỗi tinh vi gây ra bởi việc thay đổi trực tiếp.
cards.etymology: From Latin im- 'not' + mutabilis 'changeable'; see mutable.

cards.definition: Happening or existing at the same time; in computing, multiple tasks executing in overlapping time periods.
cards.exampleSentence: The server handles hundreds of concurrent requests by using a non-blocking I/O model.
cards.exampleTranslation: Máy chủ xử lý hàng trăm yêu cầu đồng thời bằng cách sử dụng mô hình I/O không chặn.
cards.etymology: From Latin concurrere 'to run together'; con- 'together' + currere 'to run'.

cards.definition: Following a particular order one step at a time, where each step must complete before the next begins.
cards.exampleSentence: Database migrations must run in sequential order to avoid schema conflicts.
cards.exampleTranslation: Các migration cơ sở dữ liệu phải chạy theo thứ tự tuần tự để tránh xung đột schema.
cards.etymology: From Medieval Latin sequentialis, from sequentia 'sequence', from sequi 'to follow'.

cards.definition: The process of hiding complex implementation details and exposing only the necessary interface to reduce complexity.
cards.exampleSentence: The repository pattern is an abstraction that decouples business logic from the database.
cards.exampleTranslation: Mô hình repository là một sự trừu tượng hóa giúp tách rời logic nghiệp vụ khỏi cơ sở dữ liệu.
cards.etymology: From Latin abstractio(n-), from abstrahere 'to draw away'; ab- + trahere 'to pull'.

cards.definition: An OOP mechanism where a class derives properties and behavior from a parent class.
cards.exampleSentence: Through inheritance, the AdminUser class automatically gets all methods defined in the User class.
cards.exampleTranslation: Thông qua tính kế thừa, lớp AdminUser tự động nhận được tất cả các phương thức được định nghĩa trong lớp User.
cards.etymology: From Old French inheritaunce, from Latin hereditare 'to inherit', from heres 'heir'.

cards.definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve a smaller instance of the same problem.
cards.exampleSentence: Traversing a file system tree is a classic use case for recursion.
cards.exampleTranslation: Duyệt cây hệ thống tập tin là một trường hợp sử dụng điển hình của đệ quy.
cards.etymology: From Latin recursio(n-) 'a running back', from recurrere 'to run back'.

cards.definition: The repetition of a process or the act of repeating steps in a loop; also a single cycle in an Agile sprint.
cards.exampleSentence: Each iteration of the for loop processes one element from the input array.
cards.exampleTranslation: Mỗi vòng lặp xử lý một phần tử từ mảng đầu vào.
cards.etymology: From Latin iteratio(n-), from iterare 'to repeat', from iterum 'again'.
